LK99-RS7: Mary Biscoe was baptized on 16 September 1619 in Little Missenden, Buckinghamshire, England, where her family resided in the Buckingham area. She lived to the age of 53, dying far from her birthplace in Boston, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony.
LHKV-Q4G: Robert Dennis was born in London, England, in 1621 and came to make his life in colonial America. By 1640 he was working in the Massachusetts Bay Colony as a carpenter and planter, and over the years he resided in Yarmouth, Portsmouth, and Woodbridge. He died in 1697 in Woodbridge Township, New Jersey, having lived 76 years.
LYDG-7DX: Sir Thomas Montgomery was born in 1650 in County Down, Ireland, and was honored as a knight. He spent time as a sailor and eventually made his way to America, where he died in Lexington, Kentucky.
MTB2-Q2B: Mary Woodear was born in 1675 in Uwchlan Township, Chester County, Pennsylvania, when the region was still British colonial America. She lived to the age of 67, dying in 1742 in Abington, near Philadelphia.
L27S-4LQ: John Michael Miller was baptized on 7 May 1752 in Stouchsburg, Berks County, Pennsylvania. He later made his home in Berkeley, Virginia, where he was recorded as a resident in 1810. He died in 1836 in Greenup, Kentucky, having lived 84 years.
GQDW-RMD: Anna Maria Margaretha Brunner was born in 1701 in Duttweiler, in the Rhineland-Palatinate region of Germany. Like many of her countrymen, she crossed the Atlantic and settled in Pennsylvania, dying in Philadelphia in 1755.
LZF8-BDZ: Hester Roome was born in 1722 in New York. She later moved south, and by 1790 she was living in Frederick, Maryland, where she died in 1800 at the age of 78.
L87D-N1Q: Pieter Willemese Roome was born in 1660 in New York City, then part of the New York Colony in British colonial America, and remained rooted there throughout his life. He was recorded as a resident of New York County in 1703 and died in the city of his birth in 1729.
K8FL-JSS: John Mills Sr was born in 1686 in Beaminster, Dorset, England.
K8KT-VJ6: Grace Meech was born in 1684 in Beaminster, Dorset, England.
L89J-B94: Anna Margaretha Sanwald was born in 1702 in Raibach, in the Württemberg region of Germany, with family ties to nearby Westheim. She lived her life in Germany, dying there in 1757 at the age of 55.
LK6Y-W39: Maria Steiner was born in 1625 in Zürich, Switzerland. She later lived in the German lands, dying in 1663 in Reihen, near Sinsheim, at the age of 38.
LHTY-R9X: Anna Hampton was born in 1650 in Salem, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony of British colonial America, and lived to the age of 76.
GXW4-W8M: Mary Ann Churchill was born in 1650 in Wraxall, Dorset, England. She crossed the Atlantic to the colonies, dying in Essex County, Virginia.
GZ5P-VVN: Mary Fullwood was born in 1665 in Coseley, Staffordshire, England, and lived to the age of 67.
L81Q-SYB: Edward Stevenson was born in 1650 on Long Island, in the New York Colony of British colonial America. He served his community as an Overseer of Newtown for two years beginning in 1683. He died in 1700 in Flushing, Queens, at the age of 50.
MF72-GP7: Henry Barnes Jr. was born in 1653 in London, England, and that same year immigrated to Charles County, Maryland, in British colonial America. He lived only to the age of 23, dying in the colony in 1676.
9VP7-5MT: Richard Ballard was born in 1702 in England and made his life in colonial Virginia, where he was a Quaker planter. He married around 1722 in Essex County and died in 1770 in Bedford, Virginia, at the age of 68.
LV76-QQ7: Sarah Clarke was born in 1651 in Newport, Rhode Island, into a prominent colonial family as the daughter of Governor Jeremiah Clark and sister of Governor Walter Clark. She lived her whole life in Newport, dying there in 1706 at the age of 55.
LWV9-5R8: Helena Eschelbacher was baptized in 1634 in the village of Eschelbach, near Sinsheim in Baden, Germany. She remained in her home village throughout her life, dying there in 1713 at the age of 79.
L45Q-W22: Elizabeth Hext was born in 1723 in Charleston, South Carolina. Records note her connection to military service on 1 January 1755. She lived to the age of 46.
KDQ7-Z2S: Maria Elisabetha Scholler was born in 1722 in Kusel, in the Bavarian region of Germany, and married there in 1741. She spent her life in Kusel, dying in her hometown in 1765 at the age of 43.
KCMJ-T62: Henrich Tobias Scholler was born in 1651 in the Palatinate region of Germany and lived to the age of 61, dying in 1712 in Kusel.
LK3R-WYX: Daniel Blondeau was born in 1629 in Saumur, in the Maine-et-Loire region of France. He emigrated across the Atlantic to New France, dying in 1702 in Charlesbourg, Québec, at the age of 73.
LKD2-LZM: Sarah Ann Jewell was born in 1620 in Waddesdon, Buckinghamshire, England. In 1646 she immigrated to Virginia in British colonial America, where she made her new home. She died there in Lower Norfolk in 1660 at the age of 40.
G8QT-PSY: James Ogilvy was born in 1515 in Airlie, Forfarshire, Scotland, and bore the noble title of Master of Ogilvy. He lived only to the age of 32, dying in 1547 at Inveresk in Midlothian.
GCZ2-ZTS: Richard Christmas was born in 1590 in London, England, and arrived in Virginia in 1634 to begin a new life in British colonial America. He resided in James City, Virginia, and his estate went to probate in York County in December 1654. He died in 1668, having lived 78 years.
L6Z8-JVN: Lady Mary Bibble was born in 1612 in Hose, Leicestershire, England, and was a member of the Quaker faith. She crossed the Atlantic to Pennsylvania, where she was connected to the Upper Darby Monthly Meeting, and died there around 1680 at the age of 68.
